sanskrit: तं विद्याद् दुःखसंयोगवियोगं योगसंज्ञितम् । स निश्चयेन योक्तव्यो योगोऽनिर्विण्णचेतसा ॥
hindiTranslation: जिसमें दुःखोंके संयोगका ही वियोग है, उसीको योग नामसे जानना चाहिये ।  वह योग जिस ध्यानयोगका लक्ष्य है, उस ध्यानयोगका अभ्यास न उकताये हुए चित्तसे निश्चयपूर्वक करना चाहिये ॥
englishTranslation: Know that as yoga: the severance of union with suffering. Practice that yoga with certainty, with an undiscouraged mind.
essence: Yoga is the breaking of suffering’s grip, practiced steadily.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: True meditation is the ending of the mind’s bond with suffering. That practice should be continued steadily, without losing heart.
simpleInsight: You can keep going without expecting every session to feel good. The practice itself is already the release.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen by grief and confusion. Krishna has already described the calm that meditation can bring, and now he names it plainly: real yoga is freedom from suffering’s grip. This verse urges steady practice without discouragement.
modernRelevance: You sit in a parked car after a hard conversation, replaying every word and wanting to avoid the next one. This verse says the way out is not escape, but steady practice without losing heart.
translationLiteral: तम् (that) / विद्यात् (one should know) / दुःखसंयोगवियोगम् (the separation from union with suffering) / योगसंज्ञितम् (called yoga) / सः (that) / निश्चयेन (with certainty) / योक्तव्यः (should be practiced) / योगः (yoga) / अनिर्विण्णचेतसा (with an undiscouraged mind)
insight: It shifts meditation from a mood you wait for to a discipline you keep practicing until suffering loosens.
